title: Traffic class parsing/writing may lose bits on truncation
description:
File: rust/lichen-ipv6/src/lib.rs:294-295 and 326

The traffic class field handling:

Writing (lines 294-295):
```rust
out[0] = 0x60 | (self.traffic_class >> 4);
out[1] = (self.traffic_class << 4) | ((self.flow_label >> 16) as u8 & 0x0f);
```

Parsing (line 326):
```rust
let traffic_class = ((buf[0] & 0x0f) << 4) | (buf[1] >> 4);
```

Analysis:
- traffic_class is u8 (8 bits)
- IPv6 header stores it split: 4 bits in byte 0, 4 bits in byte 1
- Writing: (tc >> 4) puts high 4 bits in byte 0; (tc << 4) puts low 4 bits in byte 1
- Parsing: reassembles from both bytes

This looks correct. The operations don't lose bits because traffic_class is exactly 8 bits, stored in two 4-bit segments.

Actually, let me verify line 294:
- 0x60 | (self.traffic_class >> 4)
- If traffic_class = 0xFF, traffic_class >> 4 = 0x0F
- 0x60 | 0x0F = 0x6F
- But version field should be 0110 (6), so high nibble should be 0x6
- This means traffic_class high nibble is written to low nibble of byte 0
- That's correct per IPv6 format

This is not a bug. Let me not file this.
close_reason: False positive - traffic class handling is correct. The 8-bit field is properly split across bytes 0 and 1 per IPv6 header format.
